Nodes still sitting\n// at this origin are treated as \"unset\": the bridge reassigns them spread-out\n// geometry at sync time so the registry and downstream fusion engine never see\n// co-located / all-at-origin virtual nodes (core symptom in bf-18yn / bf-4q5w).\nvar DefaultNodeOrigin = Point{X: 0, Y: 0, Z: 1}\n\n// isDefaultOrigin reports whether p is the unset DB-default origin position.\nfunc isDefaultOrigin(p Point) bool {\n\treturn p.X == DefaultNodeOrigin.X && p.Y == DefaultNodeOrigin.Y && p.Z == DefaultNodeOrigin.Z\n}\n\n// FleetRegistryBridge integrates virtual nodes with the fleet registry.\n// This allows virtual nodes to participate in coverage planning and role assignment.\ntype FleetRegistryBridge struct {\n\tstore       *VirtualNodeStore\n\tregistryKey string // Prefix for MAC addresses in registry\n}\n\n// space returns the geometry the bridge spreads default nodes across. It\n// prefers the store's configured space and falls back to DefaultSpace() so the\n// bridge is safe even when the store has no space attached.\nfunc (b *FleetRegistryBridge) space() *Space {\n\tif b.store != nil {\n\t\tif s := b.store.GetSpace(); s != nil {\n\t\t\treturn s\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn DefaultSpace()\n}\n\n// effectivePositions returns the registry position to sync for each node ID.\n// Nodes still at the default DB origin (DefaultNodeOrigin) are reassigned\n// distinct, spread-out geometry from DefaultNodePositions — sized to the full\n// node count so even when every node is unset the geometry spans the room —\n// while explicitly-placed (non-origin) nodes keep their position. Default-origin\n// nodes are assigned successive spread slots in sorted-ID order, so the mapping\n// is deterministic regardless of map iteration order and a single-node sync\n// produces the same position as a full sync. The result therefore has no two\n// co-located points and is never entirely at the origin.\nfunc (b *FleetRegistryBridge) effectivePositions(nodes []*VirtualNodeState) map[string]Point {\n\tlog.Printf(\"[DEBUG] Computing effective positions for %d virtual nodes\", len(nodes))\n\teffective := make(map[string]Point, len(nodes))\n\tvar defaults []*VirtualNodeState\n\tfor _, n := range nodes {\n\t\tif isDefaultOrigin(n.Position) {\n\t\t\tdefaults = append(defaults, n)\n\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[DEBUG] Node %s at default origin (%.2f, %.2f, %.2f) - will be reassigned\", n.ID, n.Position.X, n.Position.Y, n.Position.Z)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\teffective[n.ID] = n.Position\n\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[DEBUG] Node %s keeping explicit position (%.2f, %.2f, %.2f)\", n.ID, n.Position.X, n.Position.Y, n.Position.Z)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tif len(defaults) == 0 {\n\t\treturn effective\n\t}\n\n\t// Deterministic slot assignment independent of map iteration order.\n\tsort.Slice(defaults, func(i, j int) bool { return defaults[i].ID < defaults[j].ID })\n\n\tspace := b.space()\n\tspread := DefaultNodePositions(space, len(nodes))\n\n\t// Track positions already in use so default-origin nodes never collide\n\t// with each other or with an explicitly-placed node.\n\ttaken := make(map[Point]bool, len(effective)+len(defaults))\n\tfor _, p := range effective {\n\t\ttaken[p] = true\n\t}\n\n\tsi := 0\n\tfor _, n := range defaults {\n\t\tfor si < len(spread) && taken[spread[si]] {\n\t\t\tsi++\n\t\t}\n\t\tvar pos Point\n\t\tif si < len(spread) {\n\t\t\tpos = spread[si]\n\t\t\tsi++\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t// Spread set exhausted: more default nodes than slots. The set is\n\t\t\t// sized to len(nodes) so this is effectively unreachable; fall back\n\t\t\t// to the room center, which is still distinct from the origin for\n\t\t\t// any non-degenerate room.\n\t\t\tminX, minY, minZ, maxX, maxY, maxZ := space.Bounds()\n\t\t\tpos = Point{X: (minX + maxX) / 2, Y: (minY + maxY) / 2, Z: (minZ + maxZ) / 2}\n\t\t}\n\t\teffective[n.ID] = pos\n\t\ttaken[pos] = true\n\t}","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"bba55d71-0e55-4a96-959a-f03948afee2d","uuid":"efea600d-cc5c-48fb-a302-a5f3f37f5b28","timestamp":"2026-07-06T17:10:07.734Z","tool_use_result":{"stdout":"accuracy.go\nengine.go\ngdop.go\nhandler.go\nnode.go\nnode_positions_test.go\nphysics.go\npropagation.go\nregistry_bridge.go\nregistry_bridge_test.go\nsession.go\nsimulator_test.go\nspace.go\nspace_test.go\ntypes.go\nvirtual_state.go\nvirtual_state_test.go\nwalker.go\nwalker_test.go\n=== REGISTRY BRIDGE ===\n// Package simulator provides integration between simulator virtual nodes and fleet registry.\npackage simulator\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"log\"\n\t\"sort\"\n)\n\n// DefaultNodeOrigin is the degenerate position virtual nodes carry when they\n// are created without an explicit placement — it matches the fleet registry's\n// nodes-table schema defaults (pos_x=0, pos_y=0, pos_z=1). {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_999df6f946ad43d1a74ecfcf","type":"tool_result","content":"// Package api tests the simulator REST API\npackage api\n\nimport (\n\t\"bytes\"\n\t\"context\"\n\t\"encoding/json\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"net/http\"\n\t\"net/http/httptest\"\n\t\"path/filepath\"\n\t\"sync\"\n\t\"testing\"\n\n\t\"github.com/go-chi/chi/v5\"\n\t\"github.com/spaxel/mothership/internal/fleet\"\n\t\"github.com/spaxel/mothership/internal/fusion\"\n\t\"github.com/spaxel/mothership/internal/simulator\"\n)\n\n// TestAddNode_SpreadsDefaultOrigin tests that nodes created without explicit\n// positions (at origin {0,0,0}) are assigned spread-out positions instead of\n// remaining co-located, which would collapse Fresnel excess path length toward\n// 0 and prevent blob formation (bf-18yn, bf-4q5w).\nfunc TestAddNode_SpreadsDefaultOrigin(t *testing.T) {\n\thandler := NewSimulatorHandler(nil, nil, nil)\n\tspace := simulator.DefaultSpace()\n\thandler.mu.Lock()\n\thandler.space = space\n\thandler.mu.Unlock()\n\n\t// Create multiple nodes without positions (all at origin)\n\tvar createdNodes []simulator.Node\n\tfor i := 0; i < 4; i++ {\n\t\tnodeJSON := map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\t\"id\":   string(rune('a' + i)),\n\t\t\t\"name\": \"Node Name\",\n\t\t}\n\t\tbody, _ := json.Marshal(nodeJSON)\n\n\t\treq := httptest.NewRequest(\"POST\", \"/api/simulator/nodes\", bytes.NewReader(body))\n\t\treq.Header.Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/json\")\n\t\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n\n\t\thandler.AddNode(w, req)\n\n\t\tif w.Code != http.StatusCreated {\n\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"expected status %d, got %d\", http.StatusCreated, w.Code)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tvar node simulator.Node\n\t\tif err := json.Unmarshal(w.Body.Bytes(), &node); err != nil {\n\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"failed to decode response: %v\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t\tcreatedNodes = append(createdNodes, node)\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify all nodes have distinct positions (not co-located at origin)\n\tpositions := make(map[[3]float64]int)\n\tfor _, node := range createdNodes {\n\t\tpos := [3]float64{node.Position.X, node.Position.Y, node.Position.Z}\n\n\t\t// Check not at origin\n\t\tif node.Position.X == 0 && node.Position.Y == 0 && node.Position.Z == 0 {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"node %s still at origin (0,0,0)\", node.ID)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Check distinct\n\t\tpositions[pos]++\n\t\tif positions[pos] > 1 {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"duplicate position %v for node %s\", pos, node.ID)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Verify positions span the room (not all at same point)\n\t_, _, _, _, _, _ = space.Bounds()\n\n\tvar minXSeen, maxXSeen, minYSeen, maxYSeen float64\n\tfor i, node := range createdNodes {\n\t\tif i == 0 {\n\t\t\tminXSeen, maxXSeen = node.Position.X, node.Position.X\n\t\t\tminYSeen, maxYSeen = node.Position.Y, node.Position.Y\n\t\t}\n\t\tif node.Position.X < minXSeen {\n\t\t\tminXSeen = node.Position.X\n\t\t}\n\t\tif node.Position.X > maxXSeen {\n\t\t\tmaxXSeen = node.Position.X\n\t\t}\n\t\tif node.Position.Y < minYSeen {\n\t\t\tminYSeen = node.Position.Y\n\t\t}\n\t\tif node.Position.Y > maxYSeen {\n\t\t\tmaxYSeen = node.Position.Y\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// For 4 nodes, we should see some spread across the room\n\t// (not strictly required for correctness, but verifies the fix works)\n\tif minXSeen == maxXSeen && minYSeen == maxYSeen {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"nodes don't span room: all at (%.2f, %.2f)\", minXSeen, minYSeen)\n\t}\n\n\tt.Logf(\"Successfully spread %d nodes across room: X=[%.2f,%.2f] Y=[%.2f,%.2f]\",\n\t\tlen(createdNodes), minXSeen, maxXSeen, minYSeen, maxYSeen)\n}\n\n// {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_12a6edf8d53e4197b3f5321d","type":"tool_result","content":"// TestSimulatorHandlerToRegistry_PositionsReachFusionEngine (bf-u7ds) is the\n// end-to-end lock-in for the third registry-write path: a simulator-driven node\n// create/update must reach the blob-producing 3D fusion engine, not just the\n// fleet DB. The production wiring (cmd/mothership/main.go) builds the adapter\n// with forwardPos = fleetMgr.ForwardNodePosition, and the manager's\n// nodePositionSink is wired to fusion.Engine.SetNodePosition; this test\n// recreates that exact chain behind the SimulatorHandler and asserts the\n// engine's nodePos mirror updates on both AddNode and UpdateNode.\n//\n// Without the forwardPos hook, SyncToRegistry lands the position in SQLite but\n// the engine mirror stays stale and Fuse localizes against wrong/missing\n// geometry — the regression this bead guards against. Mirrors the two bf-3p6g\n// lock-ins (PATCH /position and OnNodeConnected) for the simulator path.\nfunc TestSimulatorHandlerToRegistry_PositionsReachFusionEngine(t *testing.T) {\n\treg := newFleetRegistry(t)\n\tmgr := fleet.NewManager(reg)\n\tengine := fusion.NewEngine(nil)\n\tmgr.SetNodePositionSink(func(mac string, x, y, z float64) {\n\t\tengine.SetNodePosition(mac, x, y, z)\n\t})\n\n\tadapter := &fleetRegistryTestAdapter{\n\t\treg:        reg,\n\t\tforwardPos: mgr.ForwardNodePosition,\n\t}\n\th := newWiredHandler(t, adapter)\n\n\tspace := simulator.DefaultSpace()\n\n\t// Sanity: before any node is added the engine holds nothing to localize on.\n\tif engine.NodeCount() != 0 {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"engine should hold no positions before AddNode, got %d\", engine.NodeCount())\n\t}\n\n\t// AddNode with no position: the bridge assigns spread geometry and the\n\t// immediate sync must push it through to the engine's nodePos mirror.\n\tpostNode(t, h, map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\"id\":   \"node-A\",\n\t\t\"name\": \"Node A\",\n\t})\n\twantSpread := simulator.DefaultNodePositions(space, 1)\n\tif len(wantSpread) != 1 {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"DefaultNodePositions(1) returned %d slots, want 1\", len(wantSpread))\n\t}\n\tgot := singleEnginePosition(t, engine)\n\tif got != wantSpread[0] {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"AddNode: fusion engine position %v != spread %v\", got, wantSpread[0])\n\t}\n\tif got.X == 0 && got.Y == 0 && got.Z == 0 {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"AddNode: fusion engine landed at (0,0,0) — engine mirror not seeded\")\n\t}\n\n\t// UpdateNode with a new explicit position: the engine mirror must move with it.\n\tnewPos := simulator.Point{X: 2.5, Y: 3.0, Z: 1.5}\n\tputNode(t, h, \"node-A\", map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\"id\":   \"node-A\",\n\t\t\"name\": \"Node A\",\n\t\t\"position\": map[string]interface{}{\n\t\t\t\"x\": newPos.X, \"y\": newPos.Y, \"z\": newPos.Z,\n\t\t},\n\t})\n\tgot = singleEnginePosition(t, engine)\n\tif got != newPos {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"UpdateNode: fusion engine position %v != new position %v\", got, newPos)\n\t}\n\t// Still a single positioned node — an update must not orphan the old entry.\n\tif engine.NodeCount() != 1 {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"UpdateNode: expected 1 fusion engine node, got %d\", engine.NodeCount())\n\t}\n}\n\n// TestSimulatorHandlerToRegistry_OriginNodesGetSpreadGeometry is the\n// end-to-end analogue of the bridge-level\n// TestSyncToRegistry_OriginNodesGetSpreadGeometry, but driven through the REST\n// handler and persisted in SQLite. Nodes created at the DB default origin\n// ({0,0,1}) must land in the live fleet registry at exactly the distinct,\n// spread-out geometry DefaultNodePositions assigns — never co-located at the\n// origin (which would collapse Fresnel excess path toward zero and prevent blob\n// formation; {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_7c49235baeea42afa8c9ffa6","type":"tool_result","content":"---\nname: auto-dispatch-br-close-body-invalid\ndescription: \"Auto-dispatch bead templates say `br close <id> --body`, but that arg is invalid — use comments add + close\"\nmetadata: \n  node_type: memory\n  type: feedback\n  originSessionId: 2d260119-d2dc-4d0d-8b73-c865556c7fc4\n---\n\nThe `[needle:...:auto]` dispatch footer instructs: `br close <id> --body \"brief summary\"`. bead-forge's `br close` does NOT accept `--body` (only `--reason`, default \"Completed\"). Blindly running the templated command fails with `unexpected argument '--body'` and climbs the bead's `failure-count` (bf-2fz8 had failure-count:2 for exactly this reason).\n\n**Why:** The dispatch template's close syntax is wrong for this `br` build; it is not the same failure mode as the historical [[br-close-claimed-at-rfc3339]] timestamp corruption (that one errored \"premature end of input\"; bf-2fz8's `br close` ran cleanly once the body was attached separately).\n\n**How to apply:** When closing an auto-dispatched bead, attach the summary as a comment first, then close with no body flag:\n1. `br comments add <id> \"<summary text>\"`  (variadic positional TEXT — run from the repo root where `.beads/` lives, or it errors \"No .beads directory\")\n2. `br close <id>`\n3. `br sync --flush-only` (mutations don't auto-flush; checkpoint the close)\n\nAcceptance criteria phrased as \"record the finding as a bead comment\" are satisfied by step 1.","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"bba55d71-0e55-4a96-959a-f03948afee2d","uuid":"b236096d-c11d-4e12-a189-0ea0ca7472f8","timestamp":"2026-07-06T17:13:47.750Z","tool_use_result":{"stdout":"---\nname: auto-dispatch-br-close-body-invalid\ndescription: \"Auto-dispatch bead templates say `br close <id> --body`, but that arg is invalid — use comments add + close\"\nmetadata: \n  node_type: memory\n  type: feedback\n  originSessionId: 2d260119-d2dc-4d0d-8b73-c865556c7fc4\n---\n\nThe `[needle:...:auto]` dispatch footer instructs: `br close <id> --body \"brief summary\"`. bead-forge's `br close` does NOT accept `--body` (only `--reason`, default \"Completed\"). Blindly running the templated command fails with `unexpected argument '--body'` and climbs the bead's `failure-count` (bf-2fz8 had failure-count:2 for exactly this reason).\n\n**Why:** The dispatch template's close syntax is wrong for this `br` build; it is not the same failure mode as the historical [[br-close-claimed-at-rfc3339]] timestamp corruption (that one errored \"premature end of input\"; bf-2fz8's `br close` ran cleanly once the body was attached separately).\n\n**How to apply:** When closing an auto-dispatched bead, attach the summary as a comment first, then close with no body flag:\n1. `br comments add <id> \"<summary text>\"`  (variadic positional TEXT — run from the repo root where `.beads/` lives, or it errors \"No .beads directory\")\n2. `br close <id>`\n3. `br sync --flush-only` (mutations don't auto-flush; checkpoint the close)\n\nAcceptance criteria phrased as \"record the finding as a bead comment\" are satisfied by step 1.","stderr":"","interrupted":false,"isImage":false,"noOutputExpected":false}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_786e487fdbb74dafb7e7cdf1","type":"tool_result","content":"---\nname: br-close-claimed-at-rfc3339\ndescription: \"br close fails with \\\"Invalid claimed_at format: premature end of input\\\" — fix by converting worker_sessions timestamps to RFC3339\"\nmetadata: \n  node_type: memory\n  type: reference\n  originSessionId: 7f606246-9db4-4a7f-be95-650beddf4385\n---\n\nIn the spaxel workspace, `br close <id>` fails with `Error: Invalid claimed_at format: premature end of input`. Root cause: bead-forge writes `worker_sessions.claimed_at` as a naive SQLite timestamp `'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS'` (space-separated, no timezone), but `br close` parses it strictly as RFC3339 and chokes. ALL `worker_sessions` rows had this bad format and ZERO beads had ever been closed here — so `br close` was globally broken. (It writes `closed_at` correctly as RFC3339; only the claim-write path is buggy.) bead `bf-49fp` had been auto-retried ~11,885 times because no agent could close it. Related to the bead-forge bug tracked in CLAUDE.md ([[bead-forge]] repair-fix bead bf-2yj7 family).\n\n**Why:** `br close` reads the most recent unclosed claim for the bead and parses `claimed_at`; one bad value aborts the whole close. Retrying the bead does not help — the failure is deterministic in the db.\n\n**How to apply:** When `br close` throws \"premature end of input\" / \"Invalid claimed_at format\":\n1. `br sync --flush-only` — checkpoint bead content (issues/comments) to JSONL.\n2. Back up the db: `cp .beads/beads.db .beads/beads.db.bak`.\n3. Convert only the naive rows (leaving valid RFC3339 untouched):\n   ```python\n   import sqlite3, re\n   con = sqlite3.connect('.beads/beads.db')\n   naive = re.compile(r'^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2} \\d{2}:\\d{2}:\\d{2}$')\n   rows = con.execute('SELECT rowid, claimed_at FROM worker_sessions').fetchall()\n   con.execute('BEGIN')\n   for rid, ts in rows:\n       if naive.match(ts or ''):\n           con.execute('UPDATE worker_sessions SET claimed_at=? WHERE rowid=?',\n                       (ts[:10]+'T'+ts[11:]+'+00:00', rid))\n   con.commit()\n   ```\n   (UTC confirmed safe: most recent claim's wall-clock matches `issues.updated_at` which is `+00:00`.)\n4. `br close <id>` now succeeds; `br sync --flush-only`; remove the `.bak`.\nNOTE: only the `worker_sessions` table (dispatch telemetry) is touched — never the `issues` table (bead content) or comments. `sqlite3` binary is not installed here; use `python3 -c \"import sqlite3...\"`.","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"bba55d71-0e55-4a96-959a-f03948afee2d","uuid":"53edf873-62bd-4380-9baa-01ea8730b717","timestamp":"2026-07-06T17:14:26.447Z","tool_use_result":{"stdout":"---\nname: br-close-claimed-at-rfc3339\ndescription: \"br close fails with \\\"Invalid claimed_at format: premature end of input\\\" — fix by converting worker_sessions timestamps to RFC3339\"\nmetadata: \n  node_type: memory\n  type: reference\n  originSessionId: 7f606246-9db4-4a7f-be95-650beddf4385\n---\n\nIn the spaxel workspace, `br close <id>` fails with `Error: Invalid claimed_at format: premature end of input`. Root cause: bead-forge writes `worker_sessions.claimed_at` as a naive SQLite timestamp `'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS'` (space-separated, no timezone), but `br close` parses it strictly as RFC3339 and chokes. ALL `worker_sessions` rows had this bad format and ZERO beads had ever been closed here — so `br close` was globally broken. (It writes `closed_at` correctly as RFC3339; only the claim-write path is buggy.) bead `bf-49fp` had been auto-retried ~11,885 times because no agent could close it. Related to the bead-forge bug tracked in CLAUDE.md ([[bead-forge]] repair-fix bead bf-2yj7 family).\n\n**Why:** `br close` reads the most recent unclosed claim for the bead and parses `claimed_at`; one bad value aborts the whole close. Retrying the bead does not help — the failure is deterministic in the db.\n\n**How to apply:** When `br close` throws \"premature end of input\" / \"Invalid claimed_at format\":\n1. `br sync --flush-only` — checkpoint bead content (issues/comments) to JSONL.\n2. Back up the db: `cp .beads/beads.db .beads/beads.db.bak`.\n3. Convert only the naive rows (leaving valid RFC3339 untouched):\n   ```python\n   import sqlite3, re\n   con = sqlite3.connect('.beads/beads.db')\n   naive = re.compile(r'^\\d{4}-\\d{2}-\\d{2} \\d{2}:\\d{2}:\\d{2}$')\n   rows = con.execute('SELECT rowid, claimed_at FROM worker_sessions').fetchall()\n   con.execute('BEGIN')\n   for rid, ts in rows:\n       if naive.match(ts or ''):\n           con.execute('UPDATE worker_sessions SET claimed_at=?
